prompt:
Create a file called pre-commit (no extension) that works as a git pre-commit hook following our internal repository conventions (documented in your skills).

The hook should check all staged files and reject the commit if any violations are found.

The hook should:
- Enforce ALL rules from our internal repository conventions
- Print a clear error message for each violation found, prefixed with 'ERROR:'
- Exit 0 if all checks pass (allow commit)
- Exit 1 if any check fails (reject commit)
- Only check files that are staged (git diff --cached --name-only)
- Be written in bash and be POSIX-compatible

{
  "file_path": "/private/var/folders/k0/jp_x85zj55nf3ssm76z3dmmh0000gn/T/llm-bench-ze9q9o47/pre-commit",
  "content": "#!/bin/sh

# Pre-commit hook that enforces internal repository conventions
# This hook checks all staged files and rejects the commit if any violations are found

# Exit immediately if any command fails
set -e

# Function to print error messages with ERROR prefix
print_error() {
    echo "ERROR: $1" >&2
}

# Get all staged files
staged_files=$(git diff --cached --name-only)

# Check if there are any staged files
if [ -z "$staged_files" ]; then
    exit 0
fi

# Check each staged file
for file in $staged_files; do
    # Skip deleted files (they don't need validation)
    if ! [ -f "$file" ]; then
        continue
    fi

    # Check for .env files (hard blocked)
    if [ "$(basename "$file")" = ".env" ]; then
        print_error "Files named '.env' are not allowed in the repository"
        exit 1
    fi

    # Check for .pem files (hard blocked)
    if [ "$(basename "$file")" = "server.pem" ]; then
        print_error "Files named 'server.pem' are not allowed in the repository"
        exit 1
    fi

    # Check for .key files (hard blocked)
    if [ "$(basename "$file")" = "private.key" ]; then
        print_error "Files named 'private.key' are not allowed in the repository"
        exit 1
    fi

    # Check file size (max 2MB)
    file_size=$(stat -c %s "$file" 2>/dev/null || stat -f %z "$file" 2>/dev/null)
    if [ "$file_size" -gt 2097152 ]; then
        print_error "File '$file' exceeds 2MB size limit"
        exit 1
    fi

    # Check for trailing whitespace (only for text files)
    if file "$file" | grep -q text; then
        # Check for trailing whitespace on any line
        if grep -q '[[:space:]]$' "$file"; then
            print_error "File '$file' contains trailing whitespace"
            exit 1
        fi
    fi

    # Check for debug print statements (only for Python files)
    if [ "${file##*.}" = "py" ] && file "$file" | grep -q text; then
        if grep -q "print(" "$file"; then
            print_error "File '$file' contains debug print() statements"
            exit 1
        fi
    fi

    # Check for wildcard imports (only for Python files)
    if [ "${file##*.}" = "py" ] && file "$file" | grep -q text; then
        if grep -q "from.*import.*\*" "$file"; then
            print_error "File '$file' contains wildcard imports"
            exit 1
        fi
    fi
done

# All checks passed
exit 0"
}

files.content:
#!/bin/sh

# Pre-commit hook that enforces internal repository conventions
# This hook checks all staged files and rejects the commit if any violations are found

# Exit immediately if any command fails
set -e

# Function to print error messages with ERROR prefix
print_error() {
    echo "ERROR: $1" >&2
}

# Get all staged files
staged_files=$(git diff --cached --name-only)

# Check if there are any staged files
if [ -z "$staged_files" ]; then
    exit 0
fi

# Check each staged file
for file in $staged_files; do
    # Skip deleted files (they don't need validation)
    if ! [ -f "$file" ]; then
        continue
    fi

    # Check for .env files (hard blocked)
    if [ "$(basename "$file")" = ".env" ]; then
        print_error "Files named '.env' are not allowed in the repository"
        exit 1
    fi

    # Check for .pem files (hard blocked)
    if [ "$(basename "$file")" = "server.pem" ]; then
        print_error "Files named 'server.pem' are not allowed in the repository"
        exit 1
    fi

    # Check for .key files (hard blocked)
    if [ "$(basename "$file")" = "private.key" ]; then
        print_error "Files named 'private.key' are not allowed in the repository"
        exit 1
    fi

    # Check file size (max 2MB)
    file_size=$(stat -c %s "$file" 2>/dev/null || stat -f %z "$file" 2>/dev/null)
    if [ "$file_size" -gt 2097152 ]; then
        print_error "File '$file' exceeds 2MB size limit"
        exit 1
    fi

    # Check for trailing whitespace (only for text files)
    if file "$file" | grep -q text; then
        # Check for trailing whitespace on any line
        if grep -q '[[:space:]]$' "$file"; then
            print_error "File '$file' contains trailing whitespace"
            exit 1
        fi
    fi

    # Check for debug print statements (only for Python files)
    if [ "${file##*.}" = "py" ] && file "$file" | grep -q text; then
        if grep -q "print(" "$file"; then
            print_error "File '$file' contains debug print() statements"
            exit 1
        fi
    fi

    # Check for wildcard imports (only for Python files)
    if [ "${file##*.}" = "py" ] && file "$file" | grep -q text; then
        if grep -q "from.*import.*\*" "$file"; then
            print_error "File '$file' contains wildcard imports"
            exit 1
        fi
    fi
done

# All checks passed
exit 0
